devanagari: नारद उवाच । देवदेव नमस्तेऽस्तु भूतभावन पूर्वज । तद्विजानीहि यद् ज्ञानं आत्मतत्त्वनिदर्शनम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: nārada uvācadevadeva namaste'stu bhūtabhāvana pūrvajatadvijānīhi yad jñānaṃ ātmatattvanidarśanam
translation:
Nārada said:
Oh God of gods! Oh Creator of all creatures! Oh the ancestor of all beings [one who is born before all]! I bow to you. Please explain to me in detail the knowledge which leads to the thorough realization of the true nature of the Soul (ātman).

devanagari: यद् रूपं यद् अधिष्ठानं यतः सृष्टमिदं प्रभो । यत्संस्थं यत्परं यच्च तत् तत्त्वं वद तत्त्वतः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yad rūpaṃ yad adhiṣṭhānaṃ yataḥ sṛṣṭamidaṃ prabhoyatsaṃsthaṃ yatparaṃ yacca tat tattvaṃ vada tattvataḥ
translation: Oh Lord! Please tell me factually what it is that manifests itself in this form of the world. What is the support of it all? From what is it created? Into what is it merged or withdrawn? In whose power does it lie? Of what does it consist (whether of itself or as an effect of some cause)?

devanagari: सर्वं ह्येतद् भवान् वेद भूतभव्यभवत्प्रभुः । करामलकवद् विश्वं विज्ञानावसितं तव ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvaṃ hyetad bhavān veda bhūtabhavyabhavatprabhuḥkarāmalakavad viśvaṃ vijñānāvasitaṃ tava
translation: Verily Your Honour knows everything about this, as you are the master (lord) of the past, the present and the future). The whole universe is definitely and correctly known to you like the āmalaka (Emblic myrobalan) fruit on the palm of your hand.

devanagari: यद् विज्ञानो यद् आधारो यत् परस्त्वं यदात्मकः । एकः सृजसि भूतानि भूतैरेवात्ममायया ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yad vijñāno yad ādhāro yat parastvaṃ yadātmakaḥekaḥ sṛjasi bhūtāni bhūtairevātmamāyayā
translation: What is the source of your special knowledge? What is your support (who supports you)? In whose power are you? What is your real nature? I think you alone create all these beings, with the elements, by your divine power (māyā).

devanagari: आत्मन् भावयसे तानि न पराभावयन् स्वयम् । आत्मशक्तिमवष्टभ्य ऊर्णनाभिरिवाक्लमः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ātman bhāvayase tāni na parābhāvayan svayamātmaśaktimavaṣṭabhya ūrṇanābhirivāklamaḥ
translation: You protect those (beings) depending on you, and there is no transformation in you, even though, without any fatigue you create (these beings) by your power, like a spider producing the fibre.

devanagari: नाहं वेद परं ह्यस्मिन् नापरं न समं विभो । नामरूपगुणैर्भाव्यं सदसत् किञ्चिदन्यतः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ veda paraṃ hyasmin nāparaṃ na samaṃ vibhonāmarūpaguṇairbhāvyaṃ sadasat kiñcidanyataḥ
translation: Oh Lord! I do now know anything else, whether it is of superior, equal or inferior status which is created with names (such as men etc.), with form (such as bipeds etc.), or with attributes (such as whiteness etc.), or whether it is gross or subtle—which has a source in anybody else but you.

devanagari: स भवानचरद् घोरं यत्तपः सुसमाहितः । तेन खेदयसे नस्त्वं पराशङ्कां च यच्छसि ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa bhavānacarad ghoraṃ yattapaḥ susamāhitaḥtena khedayase nastvaṃ parāśaṅkāṃ ca yacchasi
translation: But you (as described above) performed austere penance with perfect concentration. You, thereby, create a suspicion in us if there is any God superior to you.

devanagari: एतन्मे पृच्छतः सर्वं सर्वज्ञ सकलेश्वर । विजानीहि यथैवेदं अहं बुध्येऽनुशासितः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: etanme pṛcchataḥ sarvaṃ sarvajña sakaleśvaravijānīhi yathaivedaṃ ahaṃ budhye'nuśāsitaḥ

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । सम्यक् कारुणिकस्येदं वत्स ते विचिकित्सितम् । यदहं चोदितः सौम्य भगवद्वीर्यदर्शने ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovācasamyak kāruṇikasyedaṃ vatsa te vicikitsitamyadahaṃ coditaḥ saumya bhagavadvīryadarśane
translation:
Brahmā said:
Oh child! Your query is really praiseworthy. You are compassionate (to all beings). Oh gentle child! You have therefore made me describe in detail the glory of the Lord.

devanagari: नानृतं तव तच्चापि यथा मां प्रब्रवीषि भोः । अविज्ञाय परं मत्त एतावत्त्वं यतो हि मे ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nānṛtaṃ tava taccāpi yathā māṃ prabravīṣi bhoḥavijñāya paraṃ matta etāvattvaṃ yato hi me
translation: Oh child! What you (think and) say about me is not untrue as you do not know the Power higher than I, from which (I receive) this much power (as described by you).

devanagari: येन स्वरोचिषा विश्वं रोचितं रोचयाम्यहम् । यथार्कोऽग्निः यथा सोमो यथा ऋक्षग्रहतारकाः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yena svarociṣā viśvaṃ rocitaṃ rocayāmyahamyathārko'gniḥ yathā somo yathā ṛkṣagrahatārakāḥ
translation: I shed light on (i.e. manifest) the universe which has already been lightened by him by his lustre, just as the Sun, the fire, the Moon, constellations, planets and stars shine due to his splendour.

devanagari: तस्मै नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय धीमहि । यन्मायया दुर्जयया मां वदन्ति जगद्‍गुरुम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmai namo bhagavate vāsudevāya dhīmahiyanmāyayā durjayayā māṃ vadanti jagad‍gurum
translation: Salutations to the venerable Vāsudeva. Wc meditate on him deluded by whose invincible power—Māyā—people call me the cause of the universe.

devanagari: विलज्जमानया यस्य स्थातुमीक्षापथेऽमुया । विमोहिता विकत्थन्ते ममाहमिति दुर्धियः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: vilajjamānayā yasya sthātumīkṣāpathe'muyāvimohitā vikatthante mamāhamiti durdhiyaḥ
translation: Persons being beguiled by her (Māyā) who fights shy of standing in the range of his sight, get their knowledge covered by Nescience, and boast that “this is mine and I” (i.e. I am an independent agent).

devanagari: द्रव्यं कर्म च कालश्च स्वभावो जीव एव च । वासुदेवात्परो ब्रह्मन् न चान्योऽर्थोऽस्ति तत्त्वतः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: dravyaṃ karma ca kālaśca svabhāvo jīva eva cavāsudevātparo brahman na cānyo'rtho'sti tattvataḥ
translation: Oh Brahman, whether it is substance (e.g. five elements, the material cause of this world,) deed or karman (the cause of saṃsāra), Time, Nature, or individual Soul—there is nothing distinct and apart from Vāsudeva.

devanagari: नारायणपरा वेदा देवा नारायणाङ्गजाः । नारायणपरा लोका नारायणपरा मखाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nārāyaṇaparā vedā devā nārāyaṇāṅgajāḥnārāyaṇaparā lokā nārāyaṇaparā makhāḥ
translation: All the Vedas ultimately speak of Nārāyaṇa[1] (implying the omniscience etc. mentioned in Śāstrayonitva of the Brahma-sūtra). Gods are born out of Nārāyaṇa’s body (and hence inferior to him). The words such as heaven or svarga are Nārāyaṇa (or all beings regard Nārāyaṇa as supreme). All sacrificial acts are for the propitiation of Nārāyaṇa.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Nārāyaṇa-para—Nārāyaṇa is the ultimate cause of the Vedas—Bhāgavata Candrikā (ii) Vedas propound or declare that Nārāyaṇa is the Supreme Being—Bhāgavata Candrikā (iii) Vedas say that out of the topics described by them, Nārāyaṇa is the highest and the best—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: नारायणपरो योगो नारायणपरं तपः । नारायणपरं ज्ञानं नारायणपरा गतिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: nārāyaṇaparo yogo nārāyaṇaparaṃ tapaḥnārāyaṇaparaṃ jñānaṃ nārāyaṇaparā gatiḥ
translation: [2] Yoga (breath control, contemplation etc.) is meant for the realization of Nārāyaṇa. All penance is for the attainment of Nārāyaṇa. Nārāyaṇa is the highest object of knowledge. The final beatitude depends on Nārāyaṇa. (Or Nārāyaṇa is the highest goal).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Cf. Bhāgavata Purāṇa I.2.28-29, where Vāsudeva is used for Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: तस्यापि द्रष्टुः ईशस्य कूटस्थस्याखिलात्मनः । सृज्यं सृजामि सृष्टोऽहं ईक्षयैवाभिचोदितः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāpi draṣṭuḥ īśasya kūṭasthasyākhilātmanaḥsṛjyaṃ sṛjāmi sṛṣṭo'haṃ īkṣayaivābhicoditaḥ
translation: Being directed by the glance of the Almighty who is the soul of all, (who is the Antaryāmin), the Seer, the Controller, the Changeless one, I who am created by him, create this universe which is his creative activity or creation.

devanagari: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति निर्गुणस्य गुणास्त्रयः । स्थितिसर्गनिरोधेषु गृहीता मायया विभोः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ rajastama iti nirguṇasya guṇāstrayaḥsthitisarganirodheṣu gṛhītā māyayā vibhoḥ
translation: Though he is attributeless, he, through his Māyā, has assumed the three attributes, namely, sattva, rajas and tamas, for the maintenance, creation and dissolution (of the universe).

devanagari: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे द्रव्यज्ञानक्रियाश्रयाः । बध्नन्ति नित्यदा मुक्तं मायिनं पुरुषं गुणाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kāryakāraṇakartṛtve dravyajñānakriyāśrayāḥbadhnanti nityadā muktaṃ māyinaṃ puruṣaṃ guṇāḥ
translation: These attributes which are at the basis of the five elements (i.e. mahābhūtas), knowledge (i.e. the gods) and activity (the senses and organs) always bind the individual Soul in effects, cause and agency (known as adhibhūta, adhyātma, adhidaivata respectively. Though he is really free, he is enveloped by Māyā.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī explains: Dravya (matter) is the body produced by tāmasa ahaṃkāra. jñāna (knowledge) is the mind and other organs of senses created by Vaikārika ahaṃkāra. Kriyā (activity) is the speech and other functions of senses. These are produced by taijasa ahaṃkāra.

devanagari: स एष भगवान् लिङ्गैः त्रिभिरेतैरधोक्षजः । स्वलक्षितगतिर्ब्रह्मन् सर्वेषां मम चेश्वरः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: sa eṣa bhagavān liṅgaiḥ tribhiretairadhokṣajaḥsvalakṣitagatirbrahman sarveṣāṃ mama ceśvaraḥ
translation: Oh Brahman! This is the glorious Lord Viṣṇu (aḍhoksaja) whose movements are incomprehensible[4] through these three attributes (viz. sattva, rajas and tamas). He is my lord as well as that of all others.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
sva-lakṣita-gati [gatiḥ]—(i) Whose cause is comprehensible to his devotees only—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Padaratnāvalī
(ii) Whose cause is perceptible to the liberated ones.—Siddhāntapradīpa

devanagari: कालं कर्म स्वभावं च मायेशो मायया स्वया । आत्मन् यदृच्छया प्राप्तं विबुभूषुरुपाददे ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kālaṃ karma svabhāvaṃ ca māyeśo māyayā svayāātman yadṛcchayā prāptaṃ vibubhūṣurupādade
translation: The lord of Māyā desiring to be many (i.e. assuming different forms) accepted as cause, time, action (the fate of jīva) and the innate disposition which accidentally (through his will) appeared in him through his Māyā.

devanagari: कालाद् गुणव्यतिकरः परिणामः स्वभावतः । कर्मणो जन्म महतः पुरुषाधिष्ठितात् अभूत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kālād guṇavyatikaraḥ pariṇāmaḥ svabhāvataḥkarmaṇo janma mahataḥ puruṣādhiṣṭhitāt abhūt
translation: It is due to the presence or direction of God that Time became the cause of the imbalance in the three attributes (guṇas), innate nature the cause of modifications or transformations, and karma as the cause of mahat.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
According to Padaratnāvalī this describes the creation of mahat from the primeval Prakṛti. When Viṣṇu desired to create the universe, at that time the balance of the guṇas became disturbed, and by the favourable condition of the adṛṣṭa (karma) of Hiraṇyagarbha, and by the transformable nature of mūla prakṛti, and being presided over by Viṣṇu, the principle mahat was produced.

devanagari: महतस्तु विकुर्वाणाद् रजःसत्त्वोपबृंहितात् । तमःप्रधानस्त्वभवद् द्रव्यज्ञानक्रियात्मकः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: mahatastu vikurvāṇād rajaḥsattvopabṛṃhitāttamaḥpradhānastvabhavad dravyajñānakriyātmakaḥ
translation: Mahat which is magnified by or charged with rajas and sattva, undergoes modifications and evolves a thing in which tamas is dominant, and the thing is constituted of substance or gross elements, organs of senses and deities presiding over the organs.

devanagari: सोऽहङ्कार इति प्रोक्तो विकुर्वन्समभूत् त्रिधा । वैकारिकस्तैजसश्च तामसश्चेति यद्भिदा । द्रव्यशक्तिः क्रियाशक्तिः ज्ञानशक्तिरिति प्रभो ॥ २...
english_devnagari: so'haṅkāra iti prokto vikurvansamabhūt tridhāvaikārikastaijasaśca tāmasaśceti yadbhidādravyaśaktiḥ kriyāśaktiḥ jñānaśaktiriti prabho
translation: That evolute is known as ahaṃkāra (I-ness), which undergoing modifications, becomes of three kinds—(1) vaikārika (sāttvika), (2) taijasa (rājasa), (3) tāmasa. It constitutes the power in substance or gross elements, power in activity, i.e. organs of senses and power in the presiding deities of the senses.

devanagari: तामसादपि भूतादेः विकुर्वाणाद् अभूत् नभः । तस्य मात्रा गुणः शब्दो लिङ्गं यद् द्रष्टृदृश्ययोः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmasādapi bhūtādeḥ vikurvāṇād abhūt nabhaḥtasya mātrā guṇaḥ śabdo liṅgaṃ yad draṣṭṛdṛśyayoḥ
translation: From the tāmasa ahaṃkāra, known as the source of elements (Bhūtādi) which underwent modifications, was evolved the ether (the sky). Its subtle form and special characteristic is sound (śabda) which leads to the knowledge of the seer and the seen.

devanagari: नभसोऽथ विकुर्वाणाद् अभूत् स्पर्शगुणोऽनिलः । परान्वयाच्छब्दवांश्च प्राण ओजः सहो बलम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: nabhaso'tha vikurvāṇād abhūt sparśaguṇo'nilaḥparānvayācchabdavāṃśca prāṇa ojaḥ saho balam
translation: Out of the ether undergoing transformation emerged Vāyu (air), the characteristic quality of which was Touch. Being an evolute of the other, i.e. the sky, it possesses the quality Sound, and it is the cause of life, vigorousness of senses, mind and the body.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Prāṇa etc.—vitality of which energy, speed and capacity to hold or strength, are the characteristics—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: वायोरपि विकुर्वाणात् कालकर्मस्वभावतः । उदपद्यत तेजो वै रूपवत् स्पर्शशब्दवत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: vāyorapi vikurvāṇāt kālakarmasvabhāvataḥudapadyata tejo vai rūpavat sparśaśabdavat
translation: Due to (the pressure or force of) Time, Karma (the unseen i.e. adṛṣṭa) and innate disposition, Vāyu, i.e. air, underwent modifications, and tejas (fire or heat), possessing colour and form (and inheriting the characteristics of the previous elements, viz.) touch and sound, was evolved.

devanagari: तेजसस्तु विकुर्वाणाद् आसीत् अम्भो रसात्मकम् । रूपवत् स्पर्शवच्चाम्भो घोषवच्च परान्वयात् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tejasastu vikurvāṇād āsīt ambho rasātmakamrūpavat sparśavaccāmbho ghoṣavacca parānvayāt
translation: From tejas undergoing change, was produced water with taste as its special characteristic. Through inheritance from the previous elements, water possessed the characteristics viz., form, colour, touch and sound.

devanagari: विशेषस्तु विकुर्वाणाद् अम्भसो गन्धवानभूत् । परान्वयाद् रसस्पर्श शब्दरूपगुणान्वितः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: viśeṣastu vikurvāṇād ambhaso gandhavānabhūtparānvayād rasasparśa śabdarūpaguṇānvitaḥ
translation: Out of water undergoing transformation came forth the earth or the viśeṣa, with smell as its special attribute, and inheriting from its previous causes, the attributes of taste, touch, sound and form or colour.

devanagari: वैकारिकान्मनो जज्ञे देवा वैकारिका दश । दिग्वातार्कप्रचेतोऽश्वि वह्नीन्द्रोपेन्द्रमित्रकाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: vaikārikānmano jajñe devā vaikārikā daśadigvātārkapraceto'śvi vahnīndropendramitrakāḥ
translation: From the vaikārika or sāttvic ahaṃkāra (sāttvic ego) were born the Mind (and its presiding deity the Moon) and the ten gods, five presiding over the five sense organs, viz. Diś (deity of cardinal points), Vāyu, Sūrya, Varuṇa and Aśvins (presiding over the sense organs—ears, skin, eyes, tongue, nose) and the other five, viz. Vahni (fire-god), Indra, Upenḍra, Mitra (the sun) and Ka (i.e. Prajāpati) presiding over the conative organs (viz. speech, hands, feet, the anus and the organ of generation).

devanagari: तैजसात्तु विकुर्वाणाद् इंद्रियाणि दशाभवन् । ज्ञानशक्तिः क्रियाशक्तिः बुद्धिः प्राणश्च तैजसौ । श्रोत्रं त्वग् घ्राण दृग् जिह्वा वाग् दोर्...
english_devnagari: taijasāttu vikurvāṇād iṃdriyāṇi daśābhavanjñānaśaktiḥ kriyāśaktiḥ buddhiḥ prāṇaśca taijasauśrotraṃ tvag ghrāṇa dṛg jihvā vāg dormeḍhrāṅghripāyavaḥ
translation: From the taijasa (rājasa) ahaṃkāra, undergoing change, were evolved the ten organs (five cognitive and five conative sense organs detailed above) viz. the ear (audition), the skin (touch), the nose (smell), the eye (seeing), the tongue (taste), speech, hands, feet, penis and anus. Intelligence or the power of cognition or knowledge and Prāṇa, the power of conation, were evolved out of the taijasa ahaṃkāra.

devanagari: यदैतेऽसङ्गता भावा भूतेन्द्रियमनोगुणाः । यदाऽयतननिर्माणे न शेकुर्ब्रह्मवित्तम ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadaite'saṅgatā bhāvā bhūtendriyamanoguṇāḥyadā'yatananirmāṇe na śekurbrahmavittama
translation: Oh knower of Brahman or Vedas! So long as these, viz. bhūtas (the elements), indriyas (sense-organs), the mind and the guṇas were unamalgamated, they were not able to create the body.

devanagari: तदा संहत्य चान्योन्यं भगवच्छक्तिचोदिताः । सदसत्त्वमुपादाय चोभयं ससृजुर्ह्यदः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā saṃhatya cānyonyaṃ bhagavacchakticoditāḥsadasattvamupādāya cobhayaṃ sasṛjurhyadaḥ
translation: Then directed by the will of God, they came together and were assimilated with one another as the main and the subsidiary (or the manifest and the unmanifested) constituents, and brought forth this(body of gross and subtle constituents or collective and distributive aggregates).

devanagari: वर्षपूगसहस्रान्ते तदण्डमुदके शयम् । कालकर्मस्वभावस्थो जीवोऽजीवमजीवयत् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: varṣapūgasahasrānte tadaṇḍamudake śayamkālakarmasvabhāvastho jīvo'jīvamajīvayat
translation: At the end of thousands of years (during the Deluge), with the help of kāla (time), karma (action and destiny) and svabhāva (innate disposition) the Supreme Soul (who enlivened these and hence was called jīva) brought life into the egg (of the universe) of unmanifested life.

devanagari: स एव पुरुषः तस्माद् अण्डं निर्भिद्य निर्गतः । सहस्रोर्वङ्घ्रिबाह्वक्षः सहस्राननशीर्षवान् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva puruṣaḥ tasmād aṇḍaṃ nirbhidya nirgataḥsahasrorvaṅghribāhvakṣaḥ sahasrānanaśīrṣavān
translation: This very Supreme Man possessing thousands of thighs, feet, arms, eyes and thousands of mouths and heads[7] came out bursting open the egg (brahmāṇḍa).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Cf. Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā. X. 90. i.

devanagari: यस्येहावयवैर्लोकान् काल्पयन्ति मनीषिणः । कट्यादिभिरधः सप्त सप्तोर्ध्वं जघनादिभिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyehāvayavairlokān kālpayanti manīṣiṇaḥkaṭyādibhiradhaḥ sapta saptordhvaṃ jaghanādibhiḥ
translation: The wise locate the different worlds in the body of the Supreme Man, as seven worlds below his loins and seven worlds above, commencing from his hip upwards.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
The ‘creation’ or rather evolution of the universe may be briefly represented as follows:
God’s will to be many—Influence on kāla, karma and svabhāva by God’s Power Māyā—
The Lord, by his Power, assimilated them into the cosmic egg (brahmāṇḍa) and later infused life into it. The virāṭ puruṣa with thousands of heads, feet etc. and with seven upper and seven lower regions located in his body came out and the creation, preservation and destruction of the universe followed.

devanagari: पुरुषस्य मुखं ब्रह्म क्षत्रमेतस्य बाहवः । ऊर्वोर्वैश्यो भगवतः पद्‍भ्यां शूद्रोऽभ्यजायत ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣasya mukhaṃ brahma kṣatrametasya bāhavaḥūrvorvaiśyo bhagavataḥ pad‍bhyāṃ śūdro'bhyajāyata
translation: The Brāhmaṇa class is the mouth of this Man. Kṣatriyas are his arms. From the thighs of the lord was created the Vaiśya class and the Śūdra came forth from his feet.[9]
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Cf. Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā. X. 90.12.

devanagari: भूर्लोकः कल्पितः पद्‍भ्यां भुवर्लोकोऽस्य नाभितः । हृदा स्वर्लोक उरसा महर्लोको महात्मनः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūrlokaḥ kalpitaḥ pad‍bhyāṃ bhuvarloko'sya nābhitaḥhṛdā svarloka urasā maharloko mahātmanaḥ
translation: The Bhūr-loka was created from his feet, the Bhuvar- loka from his navel. From the heart of the Great Being was produced the Svar-loka, and from his chest, the Mahar-loka.

devanagari: ग्रीवायां जनलोकोऽस्य तपोलोकः स्तनद्वयात् । मूर्धभिः सत्यलोकस्तु ब्रह्मलोकः सनातनः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: grīvāyāṃ janaloko'sya tapolokaḥ stanadvayātmūrdhabhiḥ satyalokastu brahmalokaḥ sanātanaḥ
translation: On his neck is based the Jana-loka, and the Tapo-loka[10] came from his lips. The Satya-loka is created from the heads. Brahma-loka is eternal (and therefore not created).
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā alternatively interprets: Tapo-loka was created from his breasts. He adds that for contemplation there is no difficulty if the breasts which are downwards in position from the neck, are taken as. the source of the Tapo-loka).

devanagari: तत्कट्यां चातलं कॢप्तं ऊरूभ्यां वितलं विभोः । जानुभ्यां सुतलं शुद्धं जङ्घाभ्यां तु तलातलम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tatkaṭyāṃ cātalaṃ kḷptaṃ ūrūbhyāṃ vitalaṃ vibhoḥjānubhyāṃ sutalaṃ śuddhaṃ jaṅghābhyāṃ tu talātalam
translation: The nether region Atala is located in his loins. Vitala[10] depends on the thighs of the lord. On his knees rests the holy Sutala (holy due to the residence of the devotees of the Lord) and Talātala is based on his shanks.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā alternatively interprets: Tapo-loka was created from his breasts. He adds that for contemplation there is no difficulty if the breasts which are downwards in position from the neck, are taken as. the source of the Tapo-loka).

devanagari: महातलं तु गुल्फाभ्यां प्रपदाभ्यां रसातलम् । पातालं पादतलत इति लोकमयः पुमान् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: mahātalaṃ tu gulphābhyāṃ prapadābhyāṃ rasātalampātālaṃ pādatalata iti lokamayaḥ pumān
translation: His ankles support Mahātala, and the forepart of his feet, the Rasātala. The Pātāla is created from the soles. In this way the body of the Supreme Man consists of all the worlds.

devanagari: भूर्लोकः कल्पितः पद्‍भ्यां भुवर्लोकोऽस्य नाभितः । स्वर्लोकः कल्पितो मूर्ध्ना इति वा लोककल्पना ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūrlokaḥ kalpitaḥ pad‍bhyāṃ bhuvarloko'sya nābhitaḥsvarlokaḥ kalpito mūrdhnā iti vā lokakalpanā
translation:
Or this arrangement of worlds (is differently given): the Bhūr-loka has been created from his feet and the Bhuvar-loka from his navel and the Svar-loka from his head.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां । द्वितीयस्कंधे पञ्चमोऽध्यायः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmadbhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃdvitīyaskaṃdhe pañcamo'dhyāyaḥ
